text.source_text: अत्राह — यदुक्तं नाचेतनं प्रधानं जगत्कारणमीक्षितृत्वश्रवणादिति , तदन्यथाप्युपपद्यते । अचेतनेऽपि चेतनवदुपचारदर्शनात् । यथा प्रत्यासन्नपतनतां नद्याः कूलस्यालक्ष्य ‘ कूलं पिपतिषति ’ इत्यचेतनेऽपि कूले चेतनवदुपचारो दृष्टः , तद्वदचेतनेऽपि प्रधाने प्रत्यासन्नसर्गे चेतनवदुपचारो भविष्यति ‘ तदैक्षत ’ इति । यथा लोके कश्चिच्चेतनः ‘ स्नात्वा भुक्त्वा चापराह्णे ग्रामं रथेन गमिष्यामि ’ इतीक्षित्वा अनन्तरं तथैव नियमेन प्रवर्तते , तथा प्रधानमपि महदाद्याकारेण नियमेन प्रवर्तते । तस्माच्चेतनवदुपचर्यते । कस्मात्पुनः कारणात् विहाय मुख्यमीक्षितृत्वम् औपचारिकं तत्कल्प्यते ? ‘ तत्तेज ऐक्षत ’ (छा. उ. ६ । २ । ३) ‘ ता आप ऐक्षन्त ’ (छा. उ. ६ । २ । ४) इति चाचेतनयोरप्यप्तेजसोश्चेतनवदुपचारदर्शनात् ; तस्मात्सत्कर्तृकमपीक्षणमौपचारिकमिति गम्यते , उपचारप्राये वचनात् । इत्येवं प्राप्ते , इदं सूत्रमारभ्यते —
text.transliteration_iast: atrāha — yaduktaṃ nācetanaṃ pradhānaṃ jagatkāraṇamīkṣitṛtvaśravaṇāditi , tadanyathāpyupapadyate | acetane'pi cetanavadupacāradarśanāt | yathā pratyāsannapatanatāṃ nadyāḥ kūlasyālakṣya ‘ kūlaṃ pipatiṣati ’ ityacetane'pi kūle cetanavadupacāro dṛṣṭaḥ , tadvadacetane'pi pradhāne pratyāsannasarge cetanavadupacāro bhaviṣyati ‘ tadaikṣata ’ iti | yathā loke kaściccetanaḥ ‘ snātvā bhuktvā cāparāhṇe grāmaṃ rathena gamiṣyāmi ’ itīkṣitvā anantaraṃ tathaiva niyamena pravartate , tathā pradhānamapi mahadādyākāreṇa niyamena pravartate | tasmāccetanavadupacaryate | kasmātpunaḥ kāraṇāt vihāya mukhyamīkṣitṛtvam aupacārikaṃ tatkalpyate ? ‘ tatteja aikṣata ’ (chā. u. 6 | 2 | 3) ‘ tā āpa aikṣanta ’ (chā. u. 6 | 2 | 4) iti cācetanayorapyaptejasoścetanavadupacāradarśanāt ; tasmātsatkartṛkamapīkṣaṇamaupacārikamiti gamyate , upacāraprāye vacanāt | ityevaṃ prāpte , idaṃ sūtramārabhyate —

translation.literal_translation: Here one may object: your rejection of non-conscious Pradhana as cause based on hearing 'it saw' can be otherwise explained, since figurative conscious-language is seen even for non-conscious things. As one says of a riverbank near collapse 'the bank wants to fall,' so in non-conscious Pradhana too, when creation is near, one may have figurative conscious-language: 'it saw.' Just as in worldly case a conscious person thinks 'after bathing and eating I will go to the village' and then acts accordingly, so Pradhana too proceeds in fixed sequence as mahat etc., and thus is treated as if conscious. Why abandon primary seeing and posit figurative? Because in the same passage even non-conscious fire and water are said to 'see' ('fire saw,' 'waters saw'). Therefore sat's seeing too is figurative, since the discourse is predominantly figurative. In this obtained position, the next sutra begins -
translation.technical_translation: Purvapaksha for 1.1.6: appeals to pervasive upacara (figurative predication) in creation passage to generalize ikshana away from conscious agency.
translation.interpretive_note: Attempts to neutralize ikshateh by rhetorical leveling.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Purvapaksha cites common anthropomorphic speech to normalize non-literal agency attributions.
Illustrates assigning intention-language to non-conscious entities.
Strategy attempts to flatten all creation verbs into figurative register.
Objection weakens intentionality marker so Pradhana remains viable.
Linguistic maneuver aimed at preserving Sankhya causality in Vedantic passage.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S01 pass with stable inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit ikshateh/atman lexical precision and figurative-language boundary claims in records 1.1.032-1.1.039.
